heroHeadline: Beijing Blocks US Capital in AI Startups; CATL 6.5-Min Battery and Xpeng 7,000 Flying Car Pre-Orders Signal China's Physical AI Dominance
heroSubtitle: China instructed Moonshot AI, StepFun, and ByteDance to reject US investment without state approval (April 26) — extending the tech war from hardware controls to capital flows. CATL unveiled the Shenxing battery charging 10–98% in 6.5 minutes at Auto China 2026; BYD's 1,500 kW Flash Charging reaches 70% in 5 minutes. Xpeng's Aridge flying car drew 90+ daily orders and 7,000 total pre-orders on April 27; CEO He Xiaopeng targets FSD lead over Tesla in China by August 2026. Tesla absent from Beijing Auto Show for third consecutive year.
footerNote: Data sourced from MIIT, BIS, Bloomberg, FT, SCMP, CSET, Nikkei Asia, TechInsights, DeepSeek, Huawei Annual Reports, TrendForce, US Trade Representative, CBP, China MOFCOM, Stanford HAI AI Index 2026, House Select Committee on CCP, CNN, NPR, Al Jazeera, TechCrunch, Tom's Hardware, DigiTimes, Semafor, MIT Technology Review, CNBC, Associated Press, Yahoo Finance, PR Newswire, Electrek, CnEVPost, People's Daily Online, The Car Guide
